title: ubuntu build needs gpgme
body:
```
ubuntu@cloudhost:~$ apt-cache show buildah
Package: buildah
Priority: optional
Section: devel
Installed-Size: 12065
Maintainer: Lokesh Mandvekar <lsm5@fedoraproject.org>
Architecture: amd64
Version: 0.16-1~ubuntu16.04.2~ppa1
Depends: cri-o-runc, libseccomp2
Filename: pool/main/b/buildah/buildah_0.16-1~ubuntu16.04.2~ppa1_amd64.deb
Size: 3189772
MD5sum: aa20b745ea487188f13eabdd913d0ae4
SHA1: fab8fd47b15ebd1ba6de5feb6853d0a8b938c54f
SHA256: 43bd87aca8aa170863dcc8699793ad31f2ff0c6153fc9b6ea5978054180b3819
Description-en: A command line tool used for creating OCI images.
Description-md5: 0a460bfc5cd3241e25c0ea767bf6bc4c

ubuntu@cloudhost:~$ buildah
buildah: error while loading shared libraries: libgpgme.so.11: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory
ubuntu@cloudhost:~$
```

it'd be good if gpgme was encoded as a dependency.

This is a fresh ubuntu cloud image with me having done
```
sudo add-apt-repository ppa:projectatomic/ppa
sudo apt-get install -y buildah
```

comments.body:
@muelli Thanks for the issue.  You've probably figured this out by now, but I think you can do:
```
apt-get -y install  libgpgme11-dev
```
To get gpgme into play for you and let Buildah make progress.

I'll ask our install person extrodinaire, @lsm5 if he can add the dependency to the kit so you and others won't have to do this in the future.

only slightly related: Buildah seems to be unable to find any image:

```
syboxu@cloudhost:~$ sudo buildah --debug from fedora
sudo: unable to resolve host cloudhost: Connection timed out
DEBU[0000] overlay test mount with multiple lowers succeeded 
DEBU[0000] backingFs=extfs, projectQuotaSupported=false, useNativeDiff=true 
INFO[0000] [graphdriver] using prior storage driver: overlay 
DEBU[0000] unable to complete image name "fedora": open /etc/containers/registries.conf: no such file or directory 
DEBU[0000] parsed reference to refname into "[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/var/run/containers/storage]docker.io/library/fedora:latest" 
DEBU[0000] parsed reference to refname into "[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/var/run/containers/storage]docker.io/library/fedora:latest" 
DEBU[0000] reference "[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/var/run/containers/storage]docker.io/library/fedora:latest" does not resolve to an image ID 
DEBU[0000] parsed reference to refname into "[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/var/run/containers/storage]docker.io/library/fedora:latest" 
DEBU[0000] parsed reference to refname into "[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/var/run/containers/storage]docker.io/library/fedora:latest" 
DEBU[0000] Using registries.d directory /etc/containers/registries.d for sigstore configuration 
DEBU[0000]  No signature storage configuration found for docker.io/library/fedora:latest 
DEBU[0000] Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/docker.io 
DEBU[0000] error pulling image "fedora": error obtaining default signature policy: open /etc/containers/policy.json: no such file or directory 
DEBU[0000] error pulling and reading image "fedora": error obtaining default signature policy: open /etc/containers/policy.json: no such file or directory 
ERRO[0000] no such image "fedora" in registry: image not known 
ubuntu@cloudhost:~$ 
```

I'm unable to figure out what I can do to make it work better. The man page and the blog posts indicate that it should just work™.

Looks like you don't have a signature policy file (/etc/containers/policy.json) on your system.  That should have been included with rpm.  @lsm5 can you verify that we're adding skopeo-containers to the dependencies too please?

Short term fix is below.  This might be a bit heavy handed, I think you probably can get away with just the apt-add-repository for projectatomic then just installing skopeop-containers.
```
  apt-get -y install software-properties-common
  add-apt-repository -y ppa:alexlarsson/flatpak
  add-apt-repository -y ppa:gophers/archive
  apt-add-repository -y ppa:projectatomic/ppa
  apt-get -y -qq update
  apt-get -y install bats btrfs-tools git libapparmor-dev libdevmapper-dev libglib2.0-dev libgpgme11-dev libostree-dev libseccomp-dev libselinux1-dev skopeo-containers go-md2man
  apt-get -y install golang-1.8
```

yep, FTR: With skopeo-containers it works better.  FWIW: runc has not being pulled either. I don't know if that's considered to be bug, though. But it prevents `buildah run` from running successfully.
Yes runc should be in  the required list.

@muelli thanks for the report on deps. I'll add gpgme and skopeo-containers. RE: runc, buildah already pulls in `cri-o-runc` and not the distro's runc. @TomSweeneyRedHat how do I ensure that we're using this fork?
@muelli just built a new buildah https://launchpad.net/~projectatomic/+archive/ubuntu/ppa/+packages ... let me know how it goes
